programmes.statusNote: The current 2026-27 Programme Structure confirms 24 credit points, comprising four Core Courses (12 credit points) and four Elective Courses (12 credit points), and publishes the current course titles and credits. It does not publish a course code for any Core or Elective Course. Two shared Literature titles can be found with codes in another Programme, but that partial overlap is not evidence for the complete MACSLE code table. Keep the structure closed until the current official course catalogue or authenticated course outlines identify every code; do not infer codes from titles or publish a partial pool.

The live 2026-27 Programme and detailed-course page confirms a 24-credit-point award: all six 3-credit Core Courses, one of two 3-credit curriculum courses in Elective Group I, and one of the 3-credit Research Project or Internship in Elective Group II. Official curriculum-module pages identify seven of the ten current titles: SES6043 STEM Education: Theoretical and Curricular Perspectives, SES6044 Technology in STEM Education, SES6045 Principles and Methods in STEM Education Research, SES6046 and SES6047 for the Primary and Secondary curriculum options, SES6048 Research Project, and INT6064 Coding and Computational Thinking. No public current official source identifies the codes for Scientific Inquiry and Engineering Design, Developing Creativity and Innovation in STEM, or Internship. Keep the structure closed and do not publish a partial course pool or infer the three missing codes from departmental prefixes or numbering patterns.

The live 2026-27 Programme page confirms a 24-credit-point award: six named Core Courses carry 18 credit points in total, followed by either two courses chosen from four named Specialised Courses or the Thesis option, carrying 6 credit points in total. The page publishes all eleven current titles but no course codes and does not assign an individual credit value to each Core or Specialised Course. The current Master course catalogues of the co-delivering Early Childhood Education and Special Education and Counselling departments and the public Moodle catalogue do not identify a Programme-specific code for any of the eleven exact current titles. ECE6181 Thesis belongs to the separate Child and Family Education curriculum, while SED6030 Research Methods and Evidence-Based Practice and other legacy courses have different titles and scopes; none is evidence for the new Inclusive Early Childhood Education curriculum. Keep the structure closed until current official outlines identify every code and each individual course credit; do not map generic or similarly titled courses from another Programme onto this award.

The live 2026-27 Programme page, updated 8 July 2026, confirms a 24-credit-point award: all three 3-credit Core Courses, plus either five of eight 3-credit Elective Courses, or three Elective Courses and the 6-credit Master's Thesis. Official curriculum-module pages identify PES6250-PES6259 for the three current Core Courses and seven of the eight current Elective Courses, and the public official 2025-26 Moodle record confirms PES6260 as Master's Thesis. The Programme page names the final 3-credit Elective Course as Sports Coaching and Management for Selected Populations, but publishes no code; the public official Moodle exact-title and title-fragment searches return no matching course, and no current curriculum-module page or department course table closes that final mapping. The older official handbook uses a superseded four-Core structure and a 9-credit PES6260 thesis, so it cannot establish the revised course pool. Keep the Programme blocked and do not publish a partial elective pool, infer the missing code from the PES numbering sequence, or reuse the superseded handbook structure.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms a 24-credit award comprising four 3-credit Core Courses and four of seven 3-credit Elective Courses, and publishes every current title and credit value. The current official prospectus presents the same 12-plus-12 structure but notes that the courses offered remain subject to review. Neither source publishes the current course codes, and official searches for the named Core and Elective Courses do not locate a complete current code table or authenticated outlines. Keep the structure closed until EdUHK publishes official codes for all eleven courses; do not infer codes from similarly titled learning-sciences or educational-technology courses.

The current 2026-27 Programme page and official prospectus confirm 24 credit points: three 3-credit Core Courses, three of nine 3-credit Elective Courses, and the 6-credit Therapeutic Play Skills practicum. They publish all current titles and credits but omit the complete code table. Current official Moodle and course-sharing pages identify only a subset, including HCS6078-HCS6080, HCS6081-HCS6083, and HCS6086; the remaining cross-faculty electives are not fully mapped by a current consolidated official source. Keep the structure closed until all thirteen course codes are verified; do not publish a partial pool or infer the missing cross-faculty codes from titles.

The current 2026-27 Programme page and official prospectus confirm 24 credit points: four 3-credit Core Courses, two of four 3-credit Elective Courses, and two required 3-credit Project Courses. They publish all ten current titles and credit values but no course codes. The Programme Handbook download requires an authenticated EdUHK account, and public official Moodle searches do not expose a complete matching code table. Keep the structure closed until EdUHK publishes accessible current codes for all ten courses; do not infer codes from AI course titles or internal numbering patterns.

The live official 2026-27 Programme page confirms a 24-credit-point award comprising all four 3-credit-point Core Courses and any four of six 3-credit-point Elective Courses. Official EdUHK curriculum-module pages now confirm the code and title of every current course. INT6146 Capstone Project remains an optional Elective Course and offers the research-project and internship pathways described on the Programme page; neither pathway changes the published 3-credit value or the four-of-six elective completion rule. The full-time and part-time curriculum tables publish different study schedules but the same award requirements.

courseGroups.name: Core Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete all four Core Courses (12 credit points).

courses.name: Future of Work and Workforce Trends
Futuristic Mindset and Leadership in the Digital Age
AI for Knowledge Management and Professional Development

courseGroups.name: Elective Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: Choose any four of the six Elective Courses (12 credit points). INT6146 Capstone Project is an optional 3-credit Elective Course with either a research-project or internship pathway.

courses.name: Envision and Design Global Education Futures
Well-being and Productivity in the Digital Age
Data Mining for Trends Analysis and Forecasting
Trends in Artificial Intelligence at Workplace and at Home

programmes.statusNote: The live official 2026-27 Programme page confirms a 24-credit-point award comprising all five 3-credit Core Courses and any three of four 3-credit Elective Courses. Official EdUHK curriculum-module pages now confirm the complete current code-to-title mapping: ESG6001-ESG6005 are the five Core Courses, ESG6006-ESG6008 are three Elective options, and the shared PUA6027 is the fourth Elective option. Each course carries 3 credit points under the Programme page, so the published five-core plus three-of-four-elective completion path closes exactly without inferred codes or credits.

courseGroups.name: Core Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete all five Core Courses (15 credit points).

courseGroups.name: Elective Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: Choose any three of the four Elective Courses (9 credit points).

courses.name: Technology and Innovation Policies: Development and Trend

programmes.statusNote: The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms a two-year, 87-credit professional award comprising 22 credit points of Foundation Subjects, 42 credit points of Professional Subjects and 23 credit points of Clinical Education. It publishes all current course titles and their individual 1-8 credit values, including four Clinical Education placements totalling 23 credit points and not less than 1,000 hours, but it does not publish any course codes. Public official curriculum-module searches do not expose a complete matching code table for this new Programme. Keep the structure closed until EdUHK publishes authenticated current codes for every subject and placement; do not substitute similarly themed rehabilitation, psychology or special-education modules.

The current 2026-27 Programme page and official prospectus confirm 24 credit points: four required 3-credit Core Courses and either four 3-credit Elective Courses or two 3-credit Elective Courses plus the 6-credit Independent Research. They publish all thirteen current titles and credit values but no consolidated course code table. The overlapping Digital Governance curriculum confirms LAW6008 National Security and Informational Security, LAW6010 Cyber Criminal Law and Practice in Hong Kong, and LAW6006 Independent Research, but the other ten current National Security Law courses do not have publicly accessible official codes. Keep the structure closed until EdUHK publishes a complete current mapping; do not infer the missing LAW codes from the shared courses or numbering sequence.

The live 2026-27 Programme page confirms Programme codes A1M132/C2M059 and a 24-credit-point award comprising all five 3-credit Core Courses and three of five 3-credit Elective Courses. Official curriculum-module pages identify nine of the ten current titles: SCG6041 Curriculum and Pedagogy Innovation of School Science and Robotics, SCG6042 Assessment in Science and Robotics Education, SCG6043 Principles and Methods in Science and Robotics Education Research, SCG6044 STEAM Education Development and Innovation, SCG6045 Leading School Science and Robotics Teams and Competitions, SCG6046 Education Foundation for School Science and Robotics, SCG6047 Engineering and Programming for School Science and Robotics, SCG6048 AI in Science and Robotics Education, and SCG6049 Capstone Project. The current 2026-27 Moodle catalogue also confirms SCG6041, SCG6043 and SCG6045 as active Semester 1 offerings. No public current official source identifies the code for Digital Leadership: Transforming Teaching, Learning and Assessment. Keep the structure closed and do not publish a partial elective pool or infer the missing code from the SCG sequence or another leadership course.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms Programme codes A1M043/C2M001 and a 24-credit award completed through eight 3-credit courses. It publishes the two-Core-plus-six-Specialist route, the alternative three-Specialist-plus-Research-Project route, and ten current Areas of Focus. Several public Area pages list current Specialist Course titles, but they omit course codes; the current Core Courses, Educational Studies and English for Academic Purposes files redirect to an authenticated EdUHK SharePoint sign-in. An official public 2024-25 course-list PDF maps the older curriculum to codes, but it is not evidence that the same Core assignments and Specialist pools apply in 2026-27. Keep the structure closed until the current authenticated files or a public 2026-27 catalogue provides the complete code table and Track ownership; do not carry forward the 2024-25 mapping as the current curriculum.

The current 2026-27 Programme page confirms Programme codes A1M087/C2M019, a 24-credit award, five required 3-credit Core Courses and three 3-credit courses from one Specialisation. It confirms that English Language Education, Learning and Teaching of Chinese Language and Literature, and Curriculum Development are the three Specialisations offered in 2026-27, while Digital Approaches for Teaching Advancement and Innovative Teaching and Learning are suspended. The public curriculum-module catalogue verifies the five Core codes TLS6036, EDA6056, SED6043, PRJ6002 and PRJ6003, but the official Course List and Course Outlines page does not expose the current Specialisation course table without the restricted site flow. Keep the structure closed until all nine current Specialisation course codes and their Track ownership are publicly verifiable; do not publish the five Core Courses as a complete Programme or reuse older Specialisation lists that include suspended paths.
